passages.excerpt:
Dionysos appeared to the Greeks in vegetable form (root, tree, vine) and animal form (bull). He could influx himself into a human being and be him or her as well, as you and I know. What are names? This is the god of in-toxification, taking in poisons in a quantity small enough not to kill you but large enough for you to be lifted up to see outside space and time, and to come into a relaitonship with those vast powers who run the universe.

And then later, "...He is drunken with music, not with wine." (Jane Harrision.) By stealthy degrees Dionysos became Zagreus and then Orpheus. The trouble with Jesus is that he isn't musical. I guess they could use lipsynch, but the fact is, Jesus is Orpheus, and Christianity is the next mirthful dodge which the Loving Dodger has worked on us, those whom he loves. I have this vision of Him: much like the Hurdy-Gurdy Man: I see Him invisible to us behind us as the flock moves on, and I see small stragglers and old stragglers and stupid stragglers and sick stragglers and stragglers in general who have given up hope or lost energy and have given up to perish as the flock or pack or herd or culture or race marches on, without a look back. And sparkling with light and silently, He emerges and without breaking stride gathers up all the stragglers.

Since the flock never looks back or turns back it never sees Him. This is why Luther accurately called our God the "God of the very desperate." This is why the image of the flock and the lamb which has wandered off appear in our sacred writings, and others which refer to this archtypal rescue in stealth. Because of course if the flock knew, they'd stop trudging along and just stand or go back. They're not to know.

Now, did Dionysos let me know this because he has taken me, or did Erasmus whisper this to me in dreams because he was such a wise Christian divine and had his own theories, and I'm fronting for him? I know I'm fronting for someone. God or wise old man.

With warm personal regards,

137
a titanic fiery wind from another universe, a far vaster reality in all respects...trying to codify it, put it into linguistic categories, trying to figure it out, cope with the enormous paradoxed which effortlessly transcend and defy human reason -- priests from the time of the Cro-Magnon through Sumer, Egypt, the Greek mystery religions, on down to Calvin and Luther and Tillich -- we're all getting massive headaches and sitting up all night trying and trying to explain to ourselves and to write it down coherently...the secular world supposes that religion is a fake and a snare and we've got nothing to offer but a lot of flak talk, but in fact the reality behind the words is so far removed from what we can comprehend that our problem is really trying to reduce it and make our kind of sense out of it, and always failing, failing and never giving up, knowing what it means but never being able to get it right, never, never, always seeing something new or previously unseen, always understanding it better, giving up and then starting over, getting closer and closer; wondering if we were meant to try this. But it's a way of remembering what happened. Of recalling it. The prolonged, arduous work shows that something happened. As they say in modern semantics and philosophy: the word "banana" points to something which we call a banana but isn't, because "banana" is the word, not the thing pointed to. In this case the disparity between the words and the thing pointed to are probably the greatest possible. In 14 months I've found that my experience fits every description of personal mystic religious experience and none, every specific religion and none: each system or explanation works as well as any other, but none really is congruent; there is always a part left over, and in the night that small unexplained part or fact grows like the mustard seed or the leaven until it is the whole loaf or landscape by morning. It's as if the experience itself were alive.

If I were going to pick one tantalizing aspect I can't account for, and would give an arm and a leg to do so, it is that when my experience began I had the acute impression, absolutely real and unshakable, that I had been seeing the universe backward all my life, or somehow insideout, which is also the same as backward -- reversed, going in the "wrong way," which means that I had suddenly begun to see it not just going in the opposite direction, but correctly, at last. It wasn't just time alone going in reverse; it was like instead of being inside a sphere-like universe, I was now outside on the skin. Inside was outside, the future was controlling the past, the smallest least valuable objects assumed tremendous importance, there was solemn and vital information in near-silence. And then I read in the Gospel of Thomas where Christ says something like, "The Kingdom will come when the outer is the inner, the bigger the smaller, the man the woman," etc., except he says, "the image the image," as if that is the one constant. Maybe it's a Jungian psychological reversal of all functions and aspects of the psyche, the not-I becoming the I, etc. But -- "random" juxtopositions of writing produced meaningful --God-sent, in fact-- information. "The stone rejected by the builder," maybe, whatever that means. (The meaningless became meaningful, esp. in arrangement; and the ultimate, found in much mystical writing: the void and God were found together, as if God, when as last experienced, turned out to be nothing, which is like what Erigina used to say: Literally, God is not. Maybe we have our entire set-ground system wrong; every feature we extract through isolated scrutiny (as important) is really background, and vice versa.

[0] Jesus emphasized to the descriples that it would be to their advantage when he was crucified, because then "a second Comforter" (or Paraclete) would come who, it is implied, could be of more aid to them. What is obvious, so obvious that one overlooks it, is that the Logos qua Jesus was bound by the limitations and laws binding all men, and could not do anything "cosmic," either for his friends or for himself. It is equally obvious that the "Second Comforter" is another form of himself, but, being released by the above laws and limitations, could render aid on an unlimited basis, in ways that are transcendent and supernatural, and not the mere "magician's tricks" which Jesus was required to do, merely to show his divinity. What I see now, from reading "The Wisdom of Solomon" is simply that St. Sophia entered human form and during this time lost the various capacities (or some of them) which she had possessed as a world-builder and maintainer; it was essential that His/Her time incarnate be brief, which is exactly what Jesus says to his descriples, comparing his pain to that of childbirth. So there was Santa Sophia ranging the world, then limited to the human form, then released after the crucifixion -- to become what? There is mention in "The Wisdom of Solomon" of St. Sophia coming to good and holy men and inspiring them such as to make clear an evident similarity if not identity between the Holy Spirit (and receiving the Holy Spirit) and receiving St. Sophia. But there may be a difference. St. Sophia, prior to the incarnation, may, as is said, have been limited to coming to very special certain men, a very few saints and holy men, excluding the mass of mankind. Once, however, she had enduring and entered earthly life as we men do, then there would be no circumscription as above; she would be able, as the Holy Spirit, to range within every human soul.

The crucifixion and resurrection was a demonstration of exactly what the writer (first century B.C.) claimed for Saint Sophia, that she conferred immortality. The Logos or St. Sophia or the Holy Spirit is the key to immortality, but that is only a means (i.e. immortality) the end being to bring man closer to God. This is almost universally overlooked. Where are we going to live while immortal? In Southern California? What is meant by "immortality" must be, as Henry Vaughn figured, "to life somehow with or within or a part of God; there can be no figures in white robes; those are messengers, not departed spirits. What the whole thing is about is to draw man back into union with God; that is the ultimate goal. The means required the incarnation, because although Santa Sophia could temporarily inspire certain men, evidently she was a far-ranging plasmatic demiurge which could not be bonded to what are called "fallen" or "sinful" men. It is precisely these men whom Christ lived with, and who became available to the post-incarnation spirit which formerly (according to my theory) could not suffuse or infuse anyone but special --which is to say, a very few-- men. Once incarnated, the mystic body of her/him/it could transubstantiate this entire material world, and us as portions of it, making use of all substances here, without regard to their special piety (see Luther, on Christianity as the religion of the unfit, the unworthy, and the very damned.").

But subjectively speaking, the most astonishing events, in contrast to the great historic intervention plus the material in TEARS, were the elaborate changes made in me, end-time changes I am certain, the shaping me up to make me acceptable — for the day which in August did come (the pale white light). If there had been no historic changes or perceptions of them; if there had been no TEARS, what was left was still overwhelmingly miraculous and complicated and unprecedented...that is probably the most remarkable aspect of it: unprecedented. The only way my theory about the Parousia having arrived could be wrong would be if somehow I experienced it in ad¬ vance, a sort of mini-Parousia, but this does not accord with the fact of the great historic intervention, which was anything but mini and personal and subjective. I was brought up to —I can't say attained— certain extraordinary levels of character, as well as given certain extraordinary perceptions and insights — which fits, as an event, a sub-event, with the matrix event: the Second Coming, around which everything else revolves as planets around a sun. My involvement in this great event is certainly there, although uncertain (to me). What role did I play? Who was I? Especially the latter. Who was I historically or supernaturally? I certainly was not myself at that time; I was taken over. Those were momentous days, and therein lies the fact: uniquely momentous days in the history of the world & in my experience as well. It must be that others, many other persons, were brought around, changed, to reach their absolute best, for this occasion, but I can know that only as an article of faith; they are as mute as I. Who can I tell? Who can they tell? There must be a Christian community, a body of the faithful (the elect?) who experienced the crucial changes I experienced...but it is all internal, the changes, the judgment (or awareness of it, anyhow), and the results of that judgment, which are for the future. Perhaps we are supposed to be —remain— invisible. I have the greatest knowledge of all: that when he judged me, the Lord found me acceptable, but I also know that I was acceptable because —my godl— He stood in my place and was acceptor of the judgment in my stead I It all fits; it was He, and He judged; and as the EB says about Luther's view, when God judged me God saw His own perfect & spotless son, and so found me acceptable. But by then there was enough of me to make the judgment just. I had come to be what he had shaped me to be. And he did not do it because I was good; if I had been good he wouldn't have had to do it, change me... so no use seeking to identify my special merits. It was due to his love, his mercy. And, most of all, rescue: for someone who believed with all his heart —had faith— that someday the Paraclete would come to his door with that very rescue & redemption. But in the vision of me as the crab I saw myself as He sees me: the crab who when given or offered something turns to what it has to give something back, not to profit but to exchange in barter: an honest creature, not bright enough to take without trying to give back, perhaps; never acquiring because with each new accrition it has given back something equal, thus keeping only a constant ratio, never gain. Never besting; never getting the better of. And thus I see my whole life as it is. A virtue. I never got the hang of accrual, of profit. The man Peter, with the shaved head, at X-Kalay, to whom I gave the radio because he

This is Xtian anamnesis, a remembering of identity. Throug this we are him, not like but are. Mundane time give way to sacred time in which the original ordeal is not re-enacted but perpetually enacted; it is the archetype (in the Platonist sense), of which our ordeals are copies. The eidos is disclosed by the stripping away of linear time accretions. There is no question that this can be done, inasmuch as it was accomplished for me in 3-74. Always, the savior is manifested where not expected: "like a thief in the night." Christ is the God of the broken ("the very desperate. as Luther put it). One does not fall within his province unless the world breaks you. It is a paradox. To be broken here is to be transformed, to receive new + vastly greater life. Christ seeks in the wreckage for living material to be reshaped; thus he defeats the world as champion of what it casts down + pulverizes. The breakers in contrast need no champion (advocate) here. Theodicy is achieved not by establishing justice but by over-offsetting injustice.

I.e. the blank white paper is substituted -intervenes- for the bill of particulars that lists the sins (or crimes) for which you are being tried + punished. If the messenger arrives in time. I get the impression that the messenger is Christ. Viz: {Illustration: undulating arrow "Charges against you" has a blank paper "inserted" and a straight arrow leads to "trial"} The record is cleared by this exculpatory intervention, but it must occur before sentence is carried out; time is of the essence. I get the impression that the list of sins (crimes) are in relation to the law + involve a rigid karmic system of retributive "eye for an eye." The charges have piled up. Suddenly it occurs to me that this may not only have to do with divine punishment in some afterlife, but the karmic accrual in this life! Of course; 3-74. Christ the messenger got to me -reached me- in 2-74, just before sentence was exacted on the basis of the charges. The deterministic system can only be shorted out this way. White document -i.e. nothing written on it- would be the spotless lamb -v. Luther and the doctrine of vicarious atonement- but also eschatological judge; messenger as - yes; as in electronic circuit! The aggregate info exists but it never reaches the machinery which processes it + acts on it, due to the interrupt blank insertion. It doesn't countermand the information with counter information but with no information. It denies information, that actually exists, to the machine. The opposite of a bill of particulars would be a blank page, not a page with any other information of any kind. I am shown how the vicarious atonement works, in an information processing model. It is very simple. You just substitute a blank page for a bill of particulars (information) before it reaches the processer. This resembles Luther's conception of God seeing his spotless son instead of you on the day of judgment. In my model the retributive machinery acts automatically once it obtains the information, but it is denied the information; a deterministic system is envisioned instead of an all-knowing God ... something more like the karmic wheel. The solution is to deny it the information on which it acts before it can act, so timing is crucial; if the machinery obtains the information, it will act. __ The vicarious atonement -which is a freeing (you) from karmic determinism- has been explained to me in terms of information processing. In other words, Acts + USA 1974 are mirror opposites of each other in all respects! Acts is USA 1974 turned inside out. Literally. That is what Acts is, + that is what "Tears" tells. Overtly + cryptically, "Tears" says "Everything that seems the case is in fact not the case + vice versa." Hence when I was no longer blind I said, "I am seeing the universe inside out; up to now I'd been seeing it backward" - i.e. reversed, as in a mirror. 1) Everything that calls itself not the Empire is the Empire. 2) Everything that calls itself Xtianity is not Xtianity. 3) It is in fact the Empire. 4) The true church (as Luther said) is persecuted, hidden + poor (+, I would add, at the periphery vis-a-vis worldly power, as it was at the time + place of Acts).

Then this is why Acts + USA 1974 are 2 sets of appearances sharing a common essence. Both contain all the same ingredients, but topologically there is complete reversal in each feature, when you compare one set to the other. + Acts is true; + USA 1974 is a simulation that is a reversal of what is the case, feature by feature [...]

Constants, in such a way that at any Given moment what is happening can only really Be understood in summation of accretions of constants, not intrinsically. To declare that the apocalyptic thinkers were in essence discerning Platonic eide is now a reasonable view to take. But although this is the case, more is involved that recovery of the eide through anamnesis; an obscuring curtain has been removed - the shock to the perception is vast. The fact that we are dealing with eide (historical constants) would explain why I thought I was seeing acts (since they are constants they undergo no change).
